title: iris tenax subsp. chrysophylla
intro: Iris tenax subsp. chrysophylla is a subspecies of the iris plant, scientifically known as Iris tenax, which is a perennial flowering plant native to the western coast of North America. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its roots, which contain iridoid glycosides and other compounds that exhibit anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Iris tenax subsp. chrysophylla include its potential to alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as its ability to soothe and calm the nervous system. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used in traditional medicine to promote relaxation and relieve stress.
